conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you are responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who is 6 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Are you currently responsible for Alex? This means you're the one who looks after Alex and pays for things like food, clothes, and other essentials.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am responsible for Alex. I look after them and pay for all their food, clothes, and everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Next, I need to ask:

Do you live in the UK, and is Alex also living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives here with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Does anyone else claim Child Benefit for Alex, or is there anyone else who might also consider themselves responsible for Alex in the same way you are?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else claims Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care for an extended period (more than 12 weeks)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has been in local authority care, but not for that long - just for a couple of months.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16), meeting the age requirement
- You are responsible for Alex - you look after them and pay for their essentials
- Both you and Alex live in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- While Alex has been in local authority care, it's been for less than 12 weeks. According to the rules, you can continue to receive Child Benefit if a child is in care or hospital for up to 12 weeks

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit, so you can proceed with your claim or continue receiving it if you're already claiming.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex, looking after them and paying for their essentials. Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. While Alex has been in local authority care, it has only been for a couple of months (less than 12 weeks). According to the rules, you can continue to get Child Benefit if a child is in care or hospital for up to 12 weeks. Since Alex has been in care for less than 12 weeks, the claimant remains eligible for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: they are responsible for a child under 16 (Alex, age 6), they live in the UK, and no one else is claiming for the child. Although Alex has been in local authority care, this has been for less than the 12-week threshold, so the claimant can continue to claim Child Benefit. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
